---
title: "CA_pilot"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## CA_pilot

```{r}
# =========================================================
# Intercoder reliability script for CA_pilot.csv
# - Reads the file
# - Cleans missing values
# - Uses A1 = coder, A2 = case
# - Computes Krippendorff's alpha per variable
# - Computes simple percent agreement per variable
# - Exports results to CSV
# =========================================================

# 1) Install packages once if needed
# install.packages(c("irr", "tidyverse"))

# 2) Load packages
library(irr)
library(tidyverse)

# 3) Read the data
# Change the path if your file is stored elsewhere
dat <- read.csv("CA_pilot.csv", sep = ";", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# 4) Convert empty strings to NA
dat[dat == ""] <- NA
dat[dat == " "] <- NA

# 5) Define coder ID and case ID
# A1 = coder, A2 = case/article/unit
dat <- dat %>%
  mutate(
    coder = A1,
    case = A2
  )

# 6) Define variables to include in intercoder reliability
# Ordinal variables: ordered categories / scales
ordinal_vars <- c("C1", "C2", "C3")

# Nominal categorical variables: a few categories, but no natural order
categorical_vars <- c("A3.0", "B1", "B2", "C4", "E1")

# Binary variables: checkbox / yes-no style coded columns
binary_vars <- c(
  "D1_1", "D1_2", "D1_3",
  "D2_1", "D2_2", "D2_3", "D2_4", "D2_5", "D2_6",
  "D3_1", "D3_2"
)

# 7) Convert variables to numeric
# For binary variables, blank = 0, coded value = numeric
dat <- dat %>%
  mutate(
    across(all_of(ordinal_vars), as.numeric),
    across(all_of(binary_vars), ~ ifelse(is.na(.), 0, as.numeric(.)))
  )

# 8) Function to compute Krippendorff's alpha for one variable
# method = "ordinal" for ordinal data
# method = "nominal" for binary / unordered categories
calc_alpha <- function(data, var, method) {
  
  # Reshape from long format to wide format:
  # rows = cases, columns = coders
  wide <- data %>%
    select(case, coder, all_of(var)) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = coder, values_from = all_of(var)) %>%
    arrange(case) %>%
    select(-case)
  
  # Convert to matrix
  mat <- as.matrix(wide)
  
  # Krippendorff's alpha in irr expects raters in rows, units in columns,
  # so transpose the matrix
  alpha_out <- kripp.alpha(t(mat), method = method)
  
  # Return tidy result
  tibble(
    variable = var,
    level = method,
    alpha = alpha_out$value
  )
}

# 9) Function to compute simple percent agreement
# This checks whether all non-missing coder values match within each case
percent_agreement <- function(data, var) {
  
  wide <- data %>%
    select(case, coder, all_of(var)) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = coder, values_from = all_of(var)) %>%
    arrange(case)
  
  ratings <- wide %>% select(-case)
  
  agreement_case <- apply(ratings, 1, function(x) {
    x <- x[!is.na(x)]
    if (length(x) < 2) return(NA)   # not enough ratings to compare
    length(unique(x)) == 1          # TRUE if all coders agree
  })
  
  tibble(
    variable = var,
    percent_agreement = mean(agreement_case, na.rm = TRUE) * 100
  )
}

# 10) Run alpha for ordinal variables
ord_results <- map_dfr(ordinal_vars, ~ calc_alpha(dat, .x, method = "ordinal"))

# 11) Run alpha for nomical variables
bin_results1 <- map_dfr(binary_vars, ~ calc_alpha(dat, .x, method = "nominal"))
bin_results2 <- map_dfr(categorical_vars, ~ calc_alpha(dat, .x, method = "nominal"))

# 12) Combine alpha results
alpha_results <- bind_rows(ord_results, bin_results1, bin_results2)

# 13) Run percent agreement for all included variables
agreement_results <- map_dfr(
  c(ordinal_vars, binary_vars, categorical_vars),
  ~ percent_agreement(dat, .x)
)

# 14) Merge alpha + agreement into one table
final_results <- alpha_results %>%
  left_join(agreement_results, by = "variable") %>%
  arrange(variable)

# 15) Print results to console
print(final_results)

# 16) Save results to CSV
write.csv(final_results, "intercoder_reliability_results.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# 17) Optional: quick interpretation helper
final_results <- final_results %>%
  mutate(
    interpretation = case_when(
      alpha >= 0.80 ~ "strong",
      alpha >= 0.67 ~ "acceptable/tentative",
      alpha < 0.67  ~ "low",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

print(final_results)

# 18) Save interpreted results too
write.csv(final_results, "intercoder_reliability_results_interpreted.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# =========================================================
# NOTES
# - Excluded from this script:
#   A3  = text/string field
#   F1  = comments/free text
#   D1_0, D2_0, D3_0 = mostly empty in your file
#
# - If you want to include A3, first standardize it manually because
#   spelling differences will reduce reliability artificially.
#
# - This script assumes:
#   each case appears once per coder,
#   and coder IDs in A1 are consistent.
# =========================================================
```

```{r}
# descriptives
dat |> count(B1)

whatever = dat |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("D1"),
    names_to = "D1_full",
    values_to = "D1value"
  ) |>
  group_by(B1, D1_full, D1value) |>
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop")

whatever2 = dat |> 
  group_by(B1, E1) |> 
  summarize(n = n(), .groups = "drop")

whatever3 = dat |>
  mutate(
    C_mean = rowMeans(
      across(c(C1, C2, C3), ~ na_if(., 0)),
      na.rm = TRUE
    )
  ) |> group_by(B1) |> 
  summarise(mean_C = mean(C_mean, na.rm = TRUE))

whatever4 = dat |> 
  mutate(
    across(c(C1, C2, C3), ~ na_if(., 0)) ,
    C_score = (C1 + C2 + C3)/3, na.rm = TRUE)
```

```{r}
# t test for scale
t.test(C_score ~ B1, data = whatever4, na.rm = TRUE)
```

```{r}
#for ambiguity
dat |> group_by(B1, C4) |>
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop")
```
